prompts: Intermolecular forces affect how molecules interact based on distance. Generally, as distance decreases, intermolecular forces increase due to stronger attractions, reaching a peak at an optimal spacing. If molecules get too close, repulsive forces dominate due to overlapping electron clouds. At long distances, intermolecular attraction diminishes, becoming negligible. The strength and nature of these forces, such as dipole-dipole, hydrogen bonding, or London dispersion forces, vary. The attraction versus distance curve typically shows a decrease in interaction energy with increasing distance, emphasizing the balance between attractive and repulsive forces at different separations.
